markdown_projection.note: JSON contract is authority. No markdown projection is generated for WP-KERNEL-009 (prevents projection drift).

scope.scope: Generate markdown, HTML, plain text, wiki/Loom, and context-bundle projections from canonical rich document state. This MT belongs to RichDocumentCore. Build the canonical structured document model backing the editor, Loom, UserManual, and projections.
constraints: Use the existing microtask_plan_draft execution surface only; do not introduce a separate MT execution surface or suite field.

Core Handshake behavior must be Handshake-native: no outside app, outside server, external daemon, Docker-only path, SQLite fallback, Redis/Valkey cache authority, or sidecar paper as proof or authority.

Allowed external runtime inputs are limited to operator-provided models, GGUF/model files, tensors/safetensors, and CUI portable artifacts where the owning WP explicitly gates them; Tiptap and Monaco are allowed only as bundled/lockfile-governed product libraries integrated into Handshake.

PostgreSQL plus EventLedger is canonical for durable state, receipts, indexing evidence, runtime proof, and validation; generated markdown, wiki pages, HTML, context bundles, debug reports, and UI projections are projections only.
Every implementation handoff must be restartable by a no-context local model using only the WP/MT contract, current Master Spec bundle, product code, tests, and receipts.
Rich documents are PostgreSQL/EventLedger/CRDT-backed structured block documents, not Markdown vault authority.
All rich document state must preserve Raw/Derived/Display separation and stable entity IDs.
scope.expected_behavior: DocumentProjectionExport leaves a concrete, restartable implementation or activation artifact with PostgreSQL/EventLedger authority, Master Spec anchors, UserManual impact, and validator-readable evidence.
acceptance_criteria: DocumentProjectionExport is implemented or specified with enough detail for a no-context local model to continue from the MT alone.
The MT names exact product/governance files, state tables or APIs, constraints, failure modes, and verification evidence.
The MT rejects sidecar research-paper authority and outside-runtime proof; accepted research ideas are restated as Handshake-native requirements.
The MT handoff names HBR rows proven, blocked, or not applicable with concrete reasons.
verification: Parse the WP/MT contract and confirm this MT keeps all required fields populated.
Run the smallest relevant product, governance, or fixture test available for the touched surface; if not implemented yet, define the exact future test and negative case.
For UI/editor/UserManual surfaces, use visual debugging or declare why no GUI surface exists for this MT.
Confirm no SQLite, Docker-default, external-app, external-daemon, sidecar-paper, or mock-only proof satisfies the MT.
handoff_requirements: List files changed or intentionally left untouched.
List commands/tests run and exact pass/fail/blocker status.
List EventLedger/PostgreSQL evidence, receipt IDs, fixtures, visual-debug artifacts, and repair queues produced or expected.
List remaining risks, compatibility shims, deprecated wording, and next MTs unblocked.

evidence: Built+verified (commits b506d832 backend core + 935e0482 manual + bf0c62a5 integration tests + dd3056f5 frontend); RichDocumentCore: 6 real-PG integration + 27 unit backend tests + 8 vitest (app suite 349/349). 18 block kinds, RawDerivedDisplay (CX-100), stable KBL- ids, save/load API w/ receipts, projections (negative proof: projection-delete leaves authority byte-identical), embeds (absolute paths DB-CHECK rejected), backlinks (stable KDLNK- relationship_id), history, permissions, crash recovery; migrations 0280-0282; RichDocumentView.tsx wired to real authority with stable selectors for MT-161+ GUI groups. UserManual coverage added (1 group + 10 cmd refs).

evidence.finding: HTML projection emitted javascript:/data: hrefs (stored XSS); embeds rendered raw content_json attrs bypassing MT-152 EmbedTarget validation
evidence.fix: Strict scheme allowlist (http/https/mailto/hsk + scheme-less refs) with obfuscation-resistant scheme parsing (tab/LF/CR + control stripping, case folding); dangerous links neutralize to non-link spans; embed render routes through EmbedTarget::parse_raw with id kinds rejecting scheme-bearing values; markdown projection applies the same law
evidence.tests: lib knowledge_document 36 passed incl. html_projection_neutralizes_dangerous_href_schemes (12 obfuscated payloads), entity_encoded_scheme_stays_inert_through_escaping, html_projection_keeps_allowlisted_and_relative_link_targets, html_projection_routes_embeds_through_embed_target_validation (8 invalid shapes), markdown_projection_neutralizes_dangerous_targets_too, id_kinds_reject_scheme_bearing_values, parse_raw_infers_kind_and_applies_the_same_law, url_scheme_detection_defeats_obfuscation

findings.title: HTML projection does not sanitize URL schemes - javascript:/data: href survives into anchor href (stored XSS on click)
findings.evidence: projection.rs:151-158 typed-link href = escape_html(typed_link_target); 160-163 embed src = escape_html(embed_target); escape_html (226-239) escapes ampersand/angle/quote but NOT the URL scheme. A specLink/wpLink/fileLink/symbolLink block with attrs.target=javascript:alert(...) renders an anchor href verbatim. Precondition: the HTML projection is opened/rendered in a browser context (it is an export/preview surface).
findings.fix: Allowlist URL schemes for href/src (http,https,mailto + internal KSRC-/KRD-/spec anchors); reject or neutralize javascript:,data:,vbscript:. Render disallowed targets as inert text.

findings.title: Projection reads embed targets from raw content_json attrs, bypassing the MT-152 typed-EmbedTarget validation
findings.evidence: projection.rs:210-224 embed_target reads attrs.target then attrs.src directly off the raw node; never goes through EmbedTarget::new, so a content_json image block with attrs.src=/etc/passwd or file:/// is emitted into the projection though MT-152 claims absolute paths are impossible.
findings.fix: Route embed rendering through the validated EmbedTarget; reject/repair non-typed targets at render time.

claim_mismatches.reality: the DB CHECK guards the side table knowledge_document_embeds, NOT content_json embed blocks; projections read the unvalidated content_json attrs, so the projection path is not covered by that CHECK

missing_tests: test: render HTML projection of a doc whose link target is javascript:... and assert the scheme is neutralized
test: render a doc whose image attrs.src is an absolute path and assert it is not emitted as-is

wp_validator_review_v2.review_method: Integration Validator multi-agent workflow (wf_0ac25815-485 + wf_98386044-70a): independent per-MT reviewer + adversarial confirm on FAIL. No ACP. Governance/repo paperwork non-blocking per operator directive (2026-06-18). Bar = good code vs MT contract + WP packet + current Master Spec v02.194, plus the WP_VALIDATOR Spec-Realism Gate.
wp_validator_review_v2.summary: MT-150 (DocumentProjectionExport) requires deterministic markdown/HTML/plain-text/wiki-Loom/context-bundle projections derived from canonical rich-document state, with projections being regenerable and never authority. The code delivers a real, deterministic renderer wired into a live PG-backed API endpoint, plus a real-PostgreSQL negative proof that deleting a projection leaves the authority document byte-identical, and the adversarial-v2 stored-XSS findings are genuinely fixed (scheme allowlist + typed EmbedTarget routing). It aligns with current Master Spec v02.194 Section 2.3.13.11.
findings: projection.rs:79-92 render_projection dispatches all five required formats (Markdown, WikiLoom, Html, PlainText, ContextBundle) deterministically from the BlockTree; real renderers for 18 block kinds, no todo!()/unimplemented!()/placeholder on any path.

projection.rs:27-43 + 145-167 + 202-246: typed links and embeds are sanitized at render time. Links go through sanitize_link_target (allowlist http/https/mailto/hsk + scheme-less); javascript:/data:/vbscript:/file: neutralize to a non-link span (data-hsk-blocked-link). Embeds route through validated_embed_target -> EmbedTarget::parse_raw, so the adversarial-v2 raw-attrs bypass is closed; invalid targets render a repairable placeholder, never a live src.

embed.rs:191-219 url_scheme strips tab/LF/CR and leading controls before scheme detection, defeating browser-tolerated obfuscation (jav\tascript:, case folding). embed.rs:75-100 EmbedTarget::new rejects absolute paths, non-http url-kind, and scheme-bearing id-kinds. Comprehensive unit tests for 12+ obfuscated payloads (projection.rs:438-581, embed.rs:357-430).

api/knowledge_documents.rs:1189-1217 export_projection is a real HTTP endpoint (GET /knowledge/documents/:document_id/projection) that loads the document from PostgreSQL (get_knowledge_rich_document), rebuilds the BlockTree, and renders. Rendering never mutates authority.

tests/knowledge_document_model_tests.rs:370-446 deleting_a_projection_never_mutates_document_authority: real-PG test renders a projection, upserts it as a knowledge_wiki_projection row, snapshots authority, deletes the projection row, and asserts the authority document is byte-identical (before == after). Directly satisfies spec 2.3.13.11 'deleting a projection MUST NOT mutate authority'.

tests/knowledge_pg_support.rs:1-165: the PG harness is real PostgreSQL ONLY (POSTGRES_TEST_URL / DATABASE_URL / Handshake-managed no-Docker cluster), fresh isolated schema + full migration chain; NO SQLite/in-memory/mock fallback (returns None -> loud SKIP only when PG binaries are genuinely absent). Satisfies sub-rule-2 managed-resource proof.

Spec v02.194 line 2711 (Section 2.3.13.11) and 2725 (2.3.13.11.1) explicitly state projections (markdown/wiki/HTML/context bundles) are never authority, deletion must not mutate authority, and HTML import/sanitize must fail closed - the implementation matches this exactly.

Aside (non-blocking, paperwork): packet spec_anchors cite v02.192 and packet spec_bundle points at v02.193 while SPEC_CURRENT is v02.194; the same section numbers resolve and content is consistent, so this is stale-pointer drift only, not a code defect.
evidence_checked: git show --stat/log for commits 9527758c, bf0c62a5, b506d832

minimum_controls: No placeholders, mocks, scaffolding, or deferred-live escapes; runtime proof against Handshake-managed PostgreSQL/EventLedger where the contract names durable state.
